jokes.text: You owe me one. You all owe me one.
jokes.setup: Dwight brings bagels from NYC as a seemingly nice gesture
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals the transactional nature and expectation of payback
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - ruins the niceness immediately by making it transactional. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Actually, I had two fish stick sandwiches. My girlfriend didn't want hers, because I guess I'm the only aphrodisiac she needs.
jokes.setup: Kevin explains why he's not hungry
jokes.punchline: Awkward sexual oversharing about being an 'aphrodisiac'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Kevin - inappropriate oversharing delivered with confidence. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Fish sticks are not an aphrodisiac. You're thinking of deer penis.
jokes.setup: Kevin claims fish sticks are aphrodisiac
jokes.punchline: Dwight's matter-of-fact correction to an even more absurd alternative
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight moment - corrects Kevin with something even more ridiculous, delivered with complete authority. High craft score for originality and character integration.

jokes.text: Can't a guy just buy some bagels for his friends, so they'll owe him a favor which he can use to get someone fired who stole a co-manager position from him anymore?
jokes.setup: Dwight pretends to be hurt by cynicism about his motives
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals his exact manipulative plan in detail
jokes.explanation: Great structure - the rhetorical question format makes the reveal funnier. Classic Dwight scheming.

jokes.text: Jeez, when did everyone get so cynical?
jokes.setup: Dwight just revealed his manipulative scheme
jokes.punchline: Immediately complains about people being cynical
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that extends the irony - Dwight lacks self-awareness about his own behavior.

jokes.text: Oh, that's right, you're a woman and you need to refuse food the first time. I'll try again.
jokes.setup: Pam politely declines a bagel
jokes.punchline: Michael's sexist assumption about women's behavior around food
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate comment - perfectly in character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I've always found beauty in uncommon places. Homeless people, graffiti.
jokes.setup: Ryan shows off his photography
jokes.punchline: Pretentious statement that reveals his shallow understanding of 'art'
jokes.explanation: Pure Ryan pretentiousness - treating homeless people as art objects for his benefit.

jokes.text: There's no better medicine than birthday lunch. It'll cure all of your, 'Gee, I don't know if Michael should be dating my mother.'
jokes.setup: Michael tries to smooth over the awkward situation
jokes.punchline: Directly addressing the elephant in the room in the most awkward way possible
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt to address tension only makes it more awkward - classic Michael behavior.

jokes.text: The rest of the story has been censored due to inappropriocity.
jokes.setup: Michael starts to tell inappropriate story about giving necklace
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'inappropriocity' while being inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good Michael malapropism - creates a funny fake word while being exactly what he's warning against.

jokes.text: Because of sex? Kevin! Hey, please, Kevin. You're fired.
jokes.setup: Kevin asks if the censorship was because of sex
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate overreaction to fire Kevin
jokes.explanation: Michael's over-the-top response to Kevin's innocent question - good escalation structure.

jokes.text: Oh, man, that's crazy. Right as we were on our way out, too. I know. Isn't that always how it goes? Rarely, if ever.
jokes.setup: Jim and Pam fake emergency to avoid lunch, making generic comments
jokes.punchline: Michael's literal response kills their fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan from Michael that exposes the obviousness of Jim and Pam's lie through literal honesty.

jokes.text: Something's up. That paper was never supposed to arrive.
jokes.setup: Jim successfully gets them out of fake emergency
jokes.punchline: Talking head reveals the scheme to camera
jokes.explanation: Standard mockumentary reveal - functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Why did I get in the car? I could've struggled. I have a whistle in my purse, I didn't even blow it.
jokes.setup: Pam is stuck in uncomfortable lunch situation
jokes.punchline: Escalating regret about not using increasingly dramatic escape methods
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mild regret to treating the situation like a kidnapping - nice build.

jokes.text: Feel it against your cheek. I will.
jokes.setup: Andy polishes Dwight's briefcase as a 'favor'
jokes.punchline: Weirdly sensual instruction and Dwight's matter-of-fact agreement
jokes.explanation: Awkward interaction made funnier by both characters' complete comfort with the weirdness.

jokes.text: Do not test my politeness.
jokes.setup: Andy explains his favor-returning system
jokes.punchline: Threatening tone about being polite creates contradiction
jokes.explanation: Excellent contradiction - threatening someone about being nice. Perfect Andy character moment with great economy.

jokes.text: You know, here's a healthy fitness tip, if you clench your buttocks together while walking, you can really take the pressure off your knees.
jokes.setup: Andy continues his over-helpfulness
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate physical advice delivered as helpful tip
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy inappropriate oversharing disguised as helpfulness - in character but predictable.

jokes.text: Do not walk around with your jacket catawampus.
jokes.setup: Andy continues fixing everything about Dwight
jokes.punchline: Uses the word 'catawampus' seriously in professional context
jokes.explanation: Good word choice - 'catawampus' is funny and fits Andy's try-hard vocabulary.

jokes.text: I used to love coming here. The chicken parm is good. Big part of my childhood. Maybe Michael will start dating that, too.
jokes.setup: Pam reflects on the restaurant nostalgia
jokes.punchline: Deadpan suggestion that Michael might date the food itself
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery - takes the absurd situation to its logical extreme. High craft for originality and structure.

jokes.text: help me out with this one. Let's see, honey, you're six months pregnant, but you've only been married for 30 days, so what does that add up to?
jokes.setup: Michael's mom does math about family traditions
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive comment about Pam's pregnancy timeline
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - older generation making inappropriate observation disguised as innocent math.

jokes.text: Ooh, burn, burn on you. And a little bit on me, too.
jokes.setup: Helene makes burn about Pam's pregnancy timing
jokes.punchline: Michael celebrates burn then realizes it reflects on him too
jokes.explanation: Great Michael moment - enjoys the burn before realizing he's also implicated. Good structure with the realization beat.

jokes.text: If anything, I am robbing the grave.
jokes.setup: Michael defends age gap with 'not robbing the cradle'
jokes.punchline: Much darker alternative that's actually worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Michael thinks he's defending himself but makes it much worse. Perfect economy and structure. Strong craft score justified by originality and perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Do I really want to go snowboarding? No. But I would like to if I wanted to.
jokes.setup: Michael pressures Helene about adventurous activities
jokes.punchline: Perfectly logical but circular reasoning about wanting to want things
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic that makes perfect sense but reveals the absurdity of Michael's pressure. High craft for clever wordplay structure.

jokes.text: Gosh, these tacos are awfully complicated to make. I will make everyone's tacos!
jokes.setup: Andy and Dwight compete over being helpful
jokes.punchline: Dwight declares tacos complicated to justify taking over
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dwight's need to control everything, even simple tasks.

jokes.text: A scrapbook of our first memories, by Michael Gary Scott, for my girlfriend, Helene, on your birthday.
jokes.setup: Michael gives heartfelt gift to Helene
jokes.punchline: Over-formal title treatment for simple scrapbook
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael over-formality for simple gesture - in character but not exceptional.

jokes.text: You threw it in, I went back later, waded in, and I found it. That's amazing. It's not amazing at all. It's sort of weird that I went and did that.
jokes.setup: Michael explains retrieving penny from wishing well
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on how weird his romantic gesture actually was
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware Michael moment - recognizes his behavior is weird while still having done it.

jokes.text: And now your wish won't come true, so that blows.
jokes.setup: Michael continues explaining the penny gesture
jokes.punchline: Realizes he may have sabotaged the wish by retrieving it
jokes.explanation: Nice follow-up that extends the logic - Michael ruins his own romantic gesture through overthinking.

jokes.text: Happy Birthday, I love you, Helene.' It's really nice, Michael. I think it sucks.
jokes.setup: Michael reads plagiarized poem with personal addition
jokes.punchline: Immediately dismisses his own romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael self-sabotage - can't accept compliments on his own romantic efforts.

jokes.text: Well, that's because you and I have very different tastes, and you like lame things. It's a generational sort of gap between us.
jokes.setup: Helene compliments Michael's gift
jokes.punchline: Michael insults her taste and highlights age gap at worst moment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Michael manages to insult girlfriend while highlighting the exact issue that will break them up.

jokes.text: And then you stopped at the bar on the way back? Got thirsty.
jokes.setup: Jim was gone suspiciously long for bathroom trip
jokes.punchline: Weak excuse for obviously avoiding the situation
jokes.explanation: Simple relationship moment showing Jim's avoidance of the awkward situation - functional but mild.

jokes.text: she already knows and has done everything that I have ever wanted to do and can tell me about it. But I think I want to do those things myself.
jokes.setup: Michael reflects on relationship with older woman
jokes.punchline: Realizes he wants to experience things firsthand, not just hear about them
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of genuine Michael insight - recognizes the core issue with the age gap relationship.

jokes.text: I want you to enjoy that cake because I have something terrible I need to tell you. And I want you to enjoy your cake before I tell you this terrible, terrible thing.
jokes.setup: Michael prepares to break up with Helene
jokes.punchline: Repeatedly emphasizing how terrible the news is while forcing her to eat cake first
jokes.explanation: Great build-up that makes the situation more awkward with each repetition - good escalation structure.

jokes.text: I cannot, in good conscience, continue to date you, when I know what this is doing to her.
jokes.setup: Michael builds to breakup reason
jokes.punchline: Initially blames Pam's feelings as the reason
jokes.explanation: Sets up false reason before revealing real motivation - good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Well... Oh, thank you, Pam. And I hope you are as gracious during this next part.
jokes.setup: Michael thanks Pam for being understanding
jokes.punchline: Immediately signals there's worse news coming
jokes.explanation: Great turn - just when everyone thinks it's resolved, Michael signals more pain incoming.

jokes.text: There's another woman, and her name is Italy, and skydiving, and bungee jumping.
jokes.setup: Michael reveals there's 'another woman'
jokes.punchline: The other woman is actually abstract concepts and activities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - treats abstract desires as an actual romantic rival. Excellent craft scores justified by originality and perfect Michael character moment.

jokes.text: You need somebody who understands your references. Who is Kafka-esque? I've never... I don't know him.
jokes.setup: Michael explains incompatibility
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't know what Kafka-esque means while using it as evidence
jokes.explanation: Good Michael moment - proves his own point about cultural gaps by not understanding what he's saying.

jokes.text: And you, unfortunately, have already completed that part of your journey down there.
jokes.setup: Michael says he wants kids
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reference to Helene's fertility in cringingly inappropriate way
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael inappropriateness - discussing fertility in the worst possible euphemistic way.

jokes.text: I'm 58. What am I supposed to do now? Well, hobbies. Stop.
jokes.setup: Helene asks what she should do at her age
jokes.punchline: Michael's tone-deaf suggestion followed by Pam's sharp shutdown
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and economy - Michael's thoughtless suggestion gets immediate shutdown from Pam.

jokes.text: What if Dwight dies and I still owe him something? That is a recipe for a ghost.
jokes.setup: Kevin worries about owing Dwight favors
jokes.punchline: Logical conclusion that debt creates supernatural consequences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kevin logic - treats social debt as supernatural threat. Perfect character integration and absurdist reasoning.

jokes.text: Damn it! I am no closer to taking Jim down. What a waste of a day. I could've grown poison mushrooms that'd be this high by now!
jokes.setup: Dwight's scheme fails
jokes.punchline: Alternative activity would have been growing poison mushrooms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - casual mention of poison mushroom cultivation as normal alternative activity.

jokes.text: They're mushrooms, they don't get that high.
jokes.setup: Dwight claims he could have grown mushrooms to a certain height
jokes.punchline: Jim's literal correction about mushroom growth
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim deadpan - focuses on the least important detail to deflate Dwight's drama.

jokes.text: I have the lowest sales record of anyone here. Don't, no. It's not about numbers Pam, it's about attitude. I have the worst attitude of any person here.
jokes.setup: Michael tries to compliment Pam's work
jokes.punchline: Pam systematically refutes each compliment with honest self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Pam's honesty makes Michael's bribery attempt impossible to execute.

jokes.text: With this raise, there are strings attached. And that string is attached from my heart to your mean attitude.
jokes.setup: Michael explains conditions for the raise
jokes.punchline: Metaphor becomes literal and nonsensical connection
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt at meaningful metaphor becomes confusing word salad - classic character moment.

jokes.text: Tell me what Pam's brain is thinking. Come on, what do you want? What do you want?
jokes.setup: Michael desperately tries to bribe Pam
jokes.punchline: Addresses her face directly as separate entity from Pam
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Michael's desperation - treating Pam's face as separate negotiating entity.

jokes.text: I want to hit you. What? I want to hit you. I'll do that.
jokes.setup: Michael asks what Pam wants
jokes.punchline: Pam's simple, direct answer and Michael's immediate agreement
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and economy - Pam cuts through all of Michael's offers with simple honesty, and his immediate acceptance is perfect.

jokes.text: In the parking lot, in front of everyone. I'm going to hit you as hard as I can. Okay. Okay. See you then. See you.
jokes.setup: They negotiate the terms of Pam hitting Michael
jokes.punchline: Treating physical violence like a business meeting with polite goodbyes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast - the formal politeness applied to arranging violence. Great escalation of the absurd situation.

jokes.text: Pam has that crazy pregnancy strength now. I'm pretty sure we said slap. No, it's a punch
jokes.setup: Office discusses the upcoming confrontation
jokes.punchline: Details keep getting worse for Michael as reality sets in
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Michael's situation keeps getting worse as details are clarified.

jokes.text: Do you and Pam ever get frisky? Inappropriate. I mean, do you ever wrestle with each other?
jokes.setup: Michael seeks information about Pam's fighting ability
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate question barely disguised with slight rewording
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael - acknowledges inappropriateness then immediately does it anyway with slight modification.

jokes.text: She wants it bad, Michael.
jokes.setup: Jim discusses Pam's desire to hit Michael
jokes.punchline: Phrasing sounds sexual but refers to violence
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - plays on the earlier inappropriate question with double meaning.

jokes.text: Every day, weirdos pay dominatrixes hundreds of dollars for that very privilege. I'm scared I'm going to love it.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if he's scared of getting hit
jokes.punchline: Connects situation to BDSM and fears he might enjoy it
jokes.explanation: Dark subversive humor - Michael's logic takes an unexpected turn that's both uncomfortable and funny.

jokes.text: Are you eating popcorn? It has almost no calories.
jokes.setup: Michael notices Kevin eating during the confrontation setup
jokes.punchline: Kevin's health-conscious justification for treating violence as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin character moment - treats the situation as entertainment while being health-conscious.

jokes.text: For the record, your mom came on to me.
jokes.setup: Michael apologizes and promises not to date family members
jokes.punchline: Immediately adds inflammatory detail that makes everything worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - can't help but make things worse right when they're resolved. Great timing and escalation.

jokes.text: Holy crap!
jokes.setup: Pam punches Michael after his inflammatory comment
jokes.punchline: Michael's shocked reaction to actually getting hit hard
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff - Michael's genuine shock at the reality of the situation.

jokes.text: Feel better? No, you were right.
jokes.setup: Jim asks if hitting Michael helped
jokes.punchline: Pam's honest admission that violence didn't solve her feelings
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Pam's honesty that the cathartic violence wasn't actually cathartic.

jokes.text: As I watched Pam's big, strong hand coming toward my face, I saw my entire life flash before my eyes. And guess what? I have four kids.
jokes.setup: Michael describes life flashing before his eyes during punch
jokes.punchline: His 'life' includes things that haven't happened yet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept - Michael's life flashing before his eyes includes his imaginary future. Great originality and structure.

jokes.text: And I have a hover car and a hover house. And my wife is a runner, and it shows.
jokes.setup: Michael continues describing his imaginary future life
jokes.punchline: Mix of sci-fi fantasy and shallow physical preferences
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the absurd concept, mixing fantastical elements with Michael's shallow desires.

jokes.text: And I am rich and I never die. It doesn't sound like much, but it's enough for me.
jokes.setup: Michael continues his perfect imaginary life
jokes.punchline: Treats immortality and infinite wealth as modest aspirations
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - the massive understatement of calling immortality and infinite wealth 'not much' is perfectly Michael. High craft scores justified.

jokes.text: Fire Jim. No. I'm cashing in the favor, fire him. What are you talking... Do it.
jokes.setup: Michael owes Dwight for the chicken/ice pack
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately tries to cash in favor for his main obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - never misses an opportunity to pursue his Jim obsession, even in someone else's vulnerable moment.

jokes.text: You know what, you can keep your stupid favor. It's worthless. And this is my dinner on your face, I'm keeping it.
jokes.setup: Michael rejects all of Dwight's favor requests
jokes.punchline: Dwight declares he's keeping the chicken as his dinner
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight moment - practical even in anger, reclaiming the raw chicken for dinner rather than wasting it.
